groups._note: 5.7 unified surface model (user decision 2026-07-18): what covers the body. Gates skin_tone (skin-bearing surfaces only) and re-keys the fur/scale/feather/chassis/ethereal color groups in 35_species.json off race class, so surface decouples from race (a furless catfolk, a synthetic-skin android, a scaled hybrid). The over/full compounds absorb the retired *_coverage groups; prompts stay minimal where a color group already describes the surface.
groups.label: Surface
groups.hint: What covers the body. Gates Skin Tone (skin-bearing surfaces only) and reveals the matching fur / scale / feather / chassis / ethereal groups.

groups._note: 5.7 UI pass (user decision 2026-07-18): always visible - the conditional hide is retired. The group is the one surface-tone slot; the creator relabels it per skin_type (Fur Tone / Scale Tone / Plumage Tone / Stone Tone / Chassis Tone / Form Tone) with the same palette. fur/scale/feather color groups narrow to the *_over_skin surfaces (35_species.json) so full coverings have exactly one tone pick. Prompt fragments still read '... skin' - the per-surface prompt wording lands with the image-gen wiring session.
groups.label: Skin Tone
groups.hint: Color of the body's surface. The label follows the Surface pick - skin tone on skin, fur tone on full fur, scale tone on full scales, and so on.

groups._note: 5.7 UI pass (user decision 2026-07-18): the finish updates with the surface - complexion keys to skin-bearing surfaces (the old skin_tone gate list); fur_condition and feather_condition (35_species.json) cover the full coverings, scale_sheen and chassis_finish already exist.
groups.label: Complexion
groups.hint: Skin finish detail (matte, dewy, weathered) - a subtle render modifier. Shown for skin-bearing surfaces; fur, plumage and scale surfaces get their own condition groups.

groups.label: Hair Color
groups.hint: Primary hair color in every render.

groups._note: V2 B7: a second color set is the two-tone flag; B8 pattern appears when this is set. 5.7: hidden on bald; pattern keeps its hair_color_2 condition (one condition per group) — the server-side drop makes the chain converge.
groups.label: Second Hair Color
groups.hint: Pick a second color to make the hair two-tone - the Pattern choice appears once set.

groups.label: Color Pattern
groups.hint: How the two colors combine (gradient, streaks, tips...).

groups._note: V2 B9: length extracted from the old style list; hair_style keeps its required id and stays pure shape. 5.7: length leads the hair block (length -> style -> color), and bald hides style/colors/bangs (required-when-visible). 5.7 UI pass (user decision 2026-07-18): on the quick path - quick without required, so quick-create stays satisfiable with it unset.
groups.label: Hair Length
groups.hint: How much hair there is. Bald removes the style and color choices entirely.

groups._note: 5.7 UI pass (user decision 2026-07-18): every style carries a family class - the picker groups under class headers (the race pattern) with collapsible sections. Options reordered by family, ids untouched; seven new styles appended to their families.
groups.label: Hair Style
groups.hint: The cut and arrangement - length is set separately above. Styles are grouped by family; search or expand a group.

groups.label: Eye Color
groups.hint: Primary eye color in every render.

groups._note: V2 B14: a second color set IS the heterochromia flag.
groups.label: Heterochromia
groups.hint: Pick a second color for heterochromia (one eye each).

groups.label: Eye Shape
groups.hint: Eye silhouette - a subtle face modifier.

groups.label: Eye Features
groups.hint: Stackable eye details (glow, slit pupils, lashes...).

groups.label: Face Shape
groups.hint: Late-window detail - renders when the prompt has room.

groups.label: Lips
groups.hint: Late-window detail - renders when the prompt has room.

groups.label: Nose
groups.hint: Late-window detail - renders when the prompt has room.

groups.label: Eyebrows
groups.hint: Late-window detail - renders when the prompt has room.

groups.label: Makeup
groups.hint: Applied look - part of the character, not the scene.
